screenPlan.reviseIssueDescription:
Revising the {{screenName}} screen (`screenKey: {{screenKey}}`) of the App Blueprint.

## Revision request
{{request}}

## Guide
- This edits an existing screen, so redraw and replace the mockup under the **same screenKey (`{{screenKey}}`)** (do not bake a version/issue ID into the screenKey).
- The mockup HTML is the pure screen; write the spec, states, and interactions in the screen plan (planMarkdown).
- Once the board approves, the previous version of this screen is cleaned up automatically.
screenPlan.reviseSectionHint: Even for a finalized screen, create an issue here whenever something needs fixing.

designGuide.philosophyTemplate:
## Design Philosophy
Describe the experience this app aims to give users in a sentence or two. (e.g., lower the learner's cognitive load and let them experience small wins often.)

## Core Principles
1. One screen, one task — ask only one thing at a time.
2. Immediate feedback — a clear response to every action.
3. Peak-End — finish on a positive note.

## Voice & Tone
A warm, encouraging tone. Plain words instead of jargon.

## Do / Don't
- Do: always show progress.
- Don't: don't put more than 5 options on one screen.

help.cwd: Deprecated legacy working directory fallback for local adapters. Existing agents may still carry this value, but new configurations should use project workspaces instead.
help.promptTemplate: Sent on every heartbeat. Keep this small and dynamic. Use it for current-task framing, not large static instructions. Supports {{ agent.id }}, {{ agent.name }}, {{ agent.role }} and other template variables.

help.search: Enable Codex web search capability during runs.
help.fastMode: Enable Codex Fast mode. This burns credits/tokens much faster and is supported on GPT-5.4 and manual Codex model IDs.
help.workspaceStrategy: How Workcell should realize an execution workspace for this agent. Keep project_primary for normal cwd execution, or use git_worktree for issue-scoped isolated checkouts.
help.workspaceBaseRef: Base git ref used when creating a worktree branch. Leave blank to use the resolved workspace ref or HEAD.
help.workspaceBranchTemplate: Template for naming derived branches. Supports {{issue.identifier}}, {{issue.title}}, {{agent.name}}, {{project.id}}, {{workspace.repoRef}}, and {{slug}}.
help.worktreeParentDir: Directory where derived worktrees should be created. Absolute, ~-prefixed, and repo-relative paths are supported.
help.runtimeServicesJson: Optional workspace runtime service definitions. Use this for shared app servers, workers, or other long-lived companion processes attached to the workspace.
help.maxTurnsPerRun: Maximum number of agentic turns (tool calls) per heartbeat run.
help.command: The command to execute (e.g. node, python).
help.localCommand: Override the path to the CLI command you want the adapter to call (e.g. /usr/local/bin/claude, codex, opencode).
help.extraArgs: Extra CLI arguments for local adapters, comma-separated.
help.envVars: Environment variables injected into the adapter process. Use plain values or secret references.
help.bootstrapPrompt: Only sent when Workcell starts a fresh session. Use this for stable setup guidance that should not be repeated on every heartbeat.
help.payloadTemplateJson: Optional JSON merged into remote adapter request payloads before Workcell adds its standard wake and workspace fields.
help.webhookUrl: The URL that receives POST requests when the agent is invoked.
help.heartbeatInterval: Run this agent automatically on a timer. Useful for periodic tasks like checking for new work.
help.timeoutSec: Maximum seconds a run can take before being terminated. 0 means no timeout.
help.graceSec: Seconds to wait after sending interrupt before force-killing the process.
help.wakeOnDemand: Allow this agent to be woken by assignments, API calls, UI actions, or automated systems.
help.cooldownSec: Minimum seconds between consecutive heartbeat runs.
help.maxConcurrentRuns: Maximum number of heartbeat runs that can execute simultaneously for this agent.
help.maxTurnContinuationEnabled: Automatically queue bounded continuation runs when an adapter stops because its per-run turn cap was exhausted.
help.maxTurnContinuationMaxAttempts: Maximum automatic continuations after one max-turn stop. This is separate from max turns per run.
help.maxTurnContinuationDelaySec: Seconds to wait before starting each max-turn continuation.
help.budgetMonthlyCents: Monthly spending limit in cents. 0 means no limit.

deliberation.heading: Dual-brain (self-review)
deliberation.hint: When enabled, this agent's issue work runs in self-review rounds: the work brain (A) does the work, the review brain (B, optionally a different model) critically reviews and revises the result, and it ends once the review is OK. The same brain settings are also used for ad-hoc deliberation (the Deliberation panel).
deliberation.brainAIsAgent: This agent itself — it works with the adapter and model settings above as-is.
deliberation.reviewModeHint: Single = one review brain. Panel = several review brains review independently, then decide by consensus (majority vote) — more robust but costs more.
deliberation.differentModelNote: Tip: the review brain should use a different model/vendor than the work brain — the same model inherits its own blind spots (generator–verifier gap).
deliberation.trackHint: Auto = reason according to the work product. Artifact = run and verify it directly, judging by behavior. Research = cross-check claims against evidence.
deliberation.maxRoundsHint: Maximum number of exchanges A↔B can go without an Accept (1–8).
deliberation.pairNote: Note: this dual-brain is one agent's internal consensus. It is separate from the existing Pair setup where two agents collaborate.
deliberation.panelEmpty: Add at least one member (leaving it empty behaves like single review).
deliberation.panelMinAgreeHint: Passes once this many reviewers Accept. Leave empty for majority vote.
